mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals labor market recovery and rising household disposable income, which drives consumer spending and lifts corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the 5-day forward return distribution shows no statistical difference from the unconditional mean (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which increases corporate cash flow visibility and reduces equity risk premiums, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over any 3-year out-of-sample period, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which lowers input cost inflation and supports corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during periods of supply chain disruption.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding a 2.0 percent annualized threshold confirms economic expansion and validates corporate earnings revisions, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as institutional capital rotates into cyclical equities.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.0 percent
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if S&P 500 forward returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters where GDP growth exceeds the threshold, or if the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong nonfarm payroll growth and stagnant consumer price inflation indicates a favorable productivity shock that boosts corporate profit margins without triggering monetary tightening, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect reverses sign when CPIAUCSL rises above 3.0 percent year-over-year, or the joint signal fails to generate positive alpha relative to a market-cap weighted benchmark over any 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in retail sales volume reflects structural consumer confidence recovery, which directly supports earnings growth forecasts and compresses equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of months where retail sales show a 3-month moving average uptrend, or the rank correlation between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains at the zero lower bound while industrial production contracts, it signals forced monetary accommodation that suppresses bond yields and forces portfolio rebalancing into equities, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if equity risk premiums expand alongside falling rates during industrial contractions, or if the forward return differential between ZLB and normal rate regimes shows no statistical significance (p > 0.05).
